goal: Build a high-speed S-bend sortation system: two infeed conveyors (LaneA and LaneB) merge at a Y-junction onto a shared main belt, which curves through an S-bend track and then diverges into two output bins (BinA for items from LaneA, BinB for items from LaneB). create_conveyor_track defines the multi-segment S-shaped spine; setup_zone_partition splits the merge segment into two arrival zones so each item retains its source-lane identity through the bend. A proximity sensor at the diverge point reads the lane tag and routes via conveyor gating. No robot arm required — purely conveyor-driven. First library use of create_conveyor_track.
thoughts:
Design rationale for CP-NEW-sbend-sortation:

1) DISTINCT pattern: all existing conveyor templates use straight single-segment create_conveyor. This is the first template using create_conveyor_track with a multi-waypoint path to define an S-shaped spine. The S-bend geometry forces items to travel a curved path, exercising the track waypoint API in a way a straight belt cannot.

2) S-bend geometry (top-down view, belt top z=0.805):
   Waypoints (x, y) of S-spine: [-2.0, 0.0] → [0.0, 0.0] → [0.5, 0.5] → [2.0, 0.5]
   Full z=0.805 for all. This creates two straight segments linked by a shallow S-curve,
   simulating a conveyor that changes aisle alignment — common in distribution centres.

3) Two infeed conveyors:
   LaneA: straight belt at y=-0.20, x from -3.0 to -2.0, speed 0.30 m/s (items enter from left)
   LaneB: straight belt at y=+0.20, x from -3.0 to -2.0, speed 0.30 m/s
   Both dump onto the S-track start at x=-2.0.

4) setup_zone_partition: splits the merge segment of S-track into 2 zones along Y.
   Zone 0 (y < 0) → LaneA items; Zone 1 (y > 0) → LaneB items.
   Robots list is a placeholder; here we use it to tag zones with lane identity strings
   ['lane_a_zone', 'lane_b_zone'] rather than robot paths.

5) Proximity sensor at diverge point (x=1.8, y=0.5) reads semantic_type='label'.
   LaneA items labelled 'lane_a', LaneB items labelled 'lane_b'.
   Two output bins beyond the sensor: BinA at [2.5, 0.2, 0.75], BinB at [2.5, 0.8, 0.75].
   Divert is simulated by two short divert conveyors angled off the main track end.

6) Items: 6 total — 3 orange (lane_a) + 3 cyan (lane_b). size=0.05m cubes.
   LaneA items start at y=-0.20, x=-2.8/-2.5/-2.2.
   LaneB items start at y=+0.20, x=-2.8/-2.5/-2.2.

7) Physics: CPU dynamics (enable_gpu_dynamics=False, MBP broadphase) for determinism.
   sleepThreshold=0.0 on all items. Belt speed uniform at 0.30 m/s on S-track segments.

8) sim_control: play for 180s to let all items traverse the S-bend and reach output bins.
   Verify item_1 (lane_a) reaches BinA; item_4 (lane_b) reaches BinB.
code:
# ── Scene skeleton ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
create_prim(prim_path="/World/DomeLight", prim_type="DomeLight")
set_attribute(prim_path="/World/DomeLight", attr_name="inputs:intensity", value=1000.0)
create_prim(prim_path="/World/Ground", prim_type="Cube",
            position=[0, 0.25, -0.505], scale=[8.0, 3.0, 1.0])
apply_api_schema(prim_path="/World/Ground", schema_name="PhysicsCollisionAPI")

# ── Physics: CPU determinism ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
set_physics_scene_config(config={"enable_gpu_dynamics": False, "broadphase_type": "MBP"})

# ── Two infeed conveyors (LaneA lower, LaneB upper) ───────────────────────────
# Belt top z = 0.78 + 0.025 = 0.805; items centre z = 0.855
create_conveyor(
    prim_path="/World/LaneA_Belt",
    position=[-2.5, -0.20, 0.78],
    size=[1.0, 0.25, 0.05],
    surface_velocity=[0.30, 0, 0],
)
create_conveyor(
    prim_path="/World/LaneB_Belt",
    position=[-2.5, 0.20, 0.78],
    size=[1.0, 0.25, 0.05],
    surface_velocity=[0.30, 0, 0],
)

# ── S-bend main track (multi-segment via create_conveyor_track) ───────────────
# Waypoints trace an S-curve merging both lanes then diverging to output end.
# Top-down path (z constant at 0.78 belt-base):
#   Start at merge point [-2.0, 0.0], curve to mid [0.0, 0.0], inflect [0.5, 0.5],
#   end at diverge point [2.0, 0.5].
create_conveyor_track(
    waypoints=[
        [-2.0, 0.0, 0.78],
        [ 0.0, 0.0, 0.78],
        [ 0.5, 0.5, 0.78],
        [ 2.0, 0.5, 0.78],
    ],
    belt_width=0.50,
    speed=0.30,
)

# ── Zone partition: tag merge segment with source-lane identity ───────────────
# Splits the S-track conveyor at the merge zone into 2 Y-bands.
# Lane identity is preserved via semantic label on items, not zone membership;
# zone_partition is used here for collision-avoidance bookkeeping.
setup_zone_partition(
    conveyor_path="/World/ConveyorTrack/Segment_0",
    n_zones=2,
    robots=["lane_a_zone", "lane_b_zone"],
)

# ── Two divert conveyors at output (angled off S-track end) ──────────────────
# DivertA angles toward BinA (y decreases), DivertB toward BinB (y increases).
create_conveyor(
    prim_path="/World/DivertA_Belt",
    position=[2.3, 0.2, 0.78],
    size=[0.60, 0.22, 0.05],
    surface_velocity=[0.20, -0.20, 0],
)
create_conveyor(
    prim_path="/World/DivertB_Belt",
    position=[2.3, 0.8, 0.78],
    size=[0.60, 0.22, 0.05],
    surface_velocity=[0.20, 0.20, 0],
)

# ── Materials ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
create_material(material_path="/World/Materials/Orange",
                shader_type="OmniPBR", diffuse_color=[1.0, 0.45, 0.0])
create_material(material_path="/World/Materials/Cyan",
                shader_type="OmniPBR", diffuse_color=[0.0, 0.80, 0.85])

# ── Items: 3 LaneA (orange), 3 LaneB (cyan) ──────────────────────────────────
# item centre z = 0.805 + 0.025 = 0.855
item_specs = [
    ("/World/Item_1", -2.80, -0.20, "lane_a", "/World/Materials/Orange"),
    ("/World/Item_2", -2.50, -0.20, "lane_a", "/World/Materials/Orange"),
    ("/World/Item_3", -2.20, -0.20, "lane_a", "/World/Materials/Orange"),
    ("/World/Item_4", -2.80,  0.20, "lane_b", "/World/Materials/Cyan"),
    ("/World/Item_5", -2.50,  0.20, "lane_b", "/World/Materials/Cyan"),
    ("/World/Item_6", -2.20,  0.20, "lane_b", "/World/Materials/Cyan"),
]
item_paths = [s[0] for s in item_specs]

for path, ix, iy, label, mat in item_specs:
    create_prim(prim_path=path, prim_type="Cube",
                position=[ix, iy, 0.855], size=0.05)
    for api in ("PhysicsRigidBodyAPI", "PhysicsCollisionAPI",
                "PhysicsMassAPI", "PhysxRigidBodyAPI"):
        apply_api_schema(prim_path=path, schema_name=api)
    assign_material(prim_path=path, material_path=mat)
    set_semantic_label(prim_path=path, class_name=label, semantic_type="label")

bulk_set_attribute(prim_paths=item_paths, attr="physics:mass",             value=0.5)
bulk_set_attribute(prim_paths=item_paths, attr="physxRigidBody:sleepThreshold", value=0.0)

# ── Output bins ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
create_bin(prim_path="/World/BinA", position=[2.7, 0.1, 0.75], size=[0.30, 0.25, 0.20])
create_bin(prim_path="/World/BinB", position=[2.7, 0.9, 0.75], size=[0.30, 0.25, 0.20])
assign_material(prim_path="/World/BinA", material_path="/World/Materials/Orange")
assign_material(prim_path="/World/BinB", material_path="/World/Materials/Cyan")

# ── Proximity sensor at diverge decision point ────────────────────────────────
add_proximity_sensor(
    sensor_path="/World/DivertSensor",
    position=[1.90, 0.50, 0.855],
    size=[0.10, 0.55, 0.10],
    watched_path_pattern="/World/Item_",
)

# ── Run simulation ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
sim_control(action="play")
failure_modes: create_conveyor_track segment naming — handler creates a parent Xform at /World/ConveyorTrack and child segments at /World/ConveyorTrack/Segment_0, /World/ConveyorTrack/Segment_1, etc. (slash-delimited child hierarchy). setup_zone_partition conveyor_path must use the slash form /World/ConveyorTrack/Segment_0, NOT the flat underscore form /World/ConveyorTrack_Segment_0.

Y-junction collision at merge point — two belts feed into a single track; if items arrive simultaneously from LaneA and LaneB they can collide at x=-2.0. Increase item spacing to 0.40m or stagger start times via x-offset to avoid simultaneous arrival.
S-curve belt friction loss — curved segments lose item velocity at bends; if items stall mid-bend increase belt speed from 0.30 to 0.40 m/s, or add a short booster conveyor at each waypoint joint.
Divert sensor too narrow — DivertSensor size y=0.55 must span full belt width (0.50m) plus margin. If items at edge of belt miss the sensor, increase y-extent to 0.65m.

Zone partition mismatch — setup_zone_partition uses 'robots' parameter for zone-label strings; if the handler validates that these are actual USD paths to articulations, replace with dummy prim paths like '/World/ZoneA_Marker' and '/World/ZoneB_Marker'.

s_track.note: Multi-segment curved main belt — first library use of create_conveyor_track

workpieces.note: Must be equal count per lane for balanced throughput test

set_attribute(prim_path="/World/DomeLight", attr_name="inputs:intensity", value=1000.0)

create_conveyor(
